title: centaurea acicularis
intro: Centaurea acicularis, also known as prickly thistle, is a species of thistle that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Western North America, characterized by its prickly leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have medicinal benefits such as reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Centaurea acicularis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
